channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chevron Seeks Texas Tax Incentives for Gas Plant Supplying Data Centers
item.description: Chevron subsidiary Energy Forge One is requesting a significant tax abatement in Texas to build a natural gas power plant dedicated to powering a data center, potentially for Microsoft. The applicatio...

item.title: JAMB introduces BYOD system for UTME starting 2027 with anti-cheating measures
item.description: JAMB don announce say from 2027, dem go allow students carry their own personal computers when dem dey write UTME. Dis new system, wey dem call 'Bring Your Own Device' initiative, na one of the bigges...

item.title: Analyst Reacts to Hypothetical 25th Amendment Plan Involving Trump, Vance, and Rubio
item.description: A recent opinion piece by former Labor Secretary Robert Reich proposed a hypothetical scenario in which Vice President J.D. Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio would collaborate to remove Preside...

item.title: Filipino celebrities express concern over recent Senate controversy
item.description: Several Filipino celebrities have expressed their disappointment and concern over the recent events in the Philippine Senate, describing the incidents as embarrassing on the international stage. While...

item.title: Trump reportedly targets centrist Democrat Fetterman to maintain Senate majority
item.description: According to a report from The New Republic, former President Donald Trump has allegedly instructed Republican lawmakers to attempt persuading Democratic Senator John Fetterman of Pennsylvania to swit...

item.title: Trump and Xi Meet in Beijing to Discuss Trade and Middle East Tensions
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, marking the first U.S. presidential visit to China since 2017. The two leaders are set...

item.title: Microsoft Introduces Automatic Driver Rollbacks in Windows Update
item.description: Microsoft is introducing a new feature for Windows Update called Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery, designed to automatically revert faulty drivers to a previously verified version. Currently, when a pr...

item.title: United States Repatriates Over 450 Historical Artefacts to Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD – In a notable move strengthening cultural cooperation, the United States repatriated more than 450 valuable cultural artefacts to Pakistan in a ceremony at the Islamabad Museum on Wednesday...

item.title: Iran strengthens control over Strait of Hormuz amid tensions with UAE and Israel
item.description: Iran has escalated tensions in the Gulf region by threatening the UAE over alleged 'collusion' with Israel following Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's claims of a secret visit to the Emirate...

item.title: Oyo APC meets aspirants to discuss primaries and ensure fair selection process
item.description: The Oyo State chapter of the APC don gather all im House of Representatives and State Assembly aspirants for strategic meeting as dem dey prepare for the coming party primaries. Di meeting bring toget...

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna demands CIA return documents related to historical programs
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL), a MAGA-aligned member of Congress, issued a public warning to the CIA on Wednesday, stating that she will subpoena the agency if it does not return specific documents to...

item.title: Kevin Warsh’s Potential Independence as Federal Reserve Chair Amid Political Pressure
item.description: Kevin Warsh is expected to be confirmed as the next Federal Reserve chair on May 13, 2026, positioning him as one of the most influential central bankers globally. During his Senate confirmation heari...

item.title: Senate Shooting Suspect Identified as Volunteer, Not Official NBI Employee
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) clarified that the driver arrested following the Senate shooting incident on Wednesday night was not an official employee of the agency. NBI Director Melvin ...

item.title: Marine Shares a Moment of Humanity With a Goat Amid Saipan Battle
item.description: In a striking photograph from July 1944, Maine First Sergeant Neil I. Shober of Fort Wayne, Indiana, is seen offering a banana to a native goat during the Battle of Saipan. Captured by combat photogra...

item.title: Wes Streeting Considers Labour Leadership Bid Amid Starmer’s Hold on Party
item.description: As the King’s Speech approached, speculation grew around Labour’s health secretary, Wes Streeting, and his potential bid for the party leadership. For some, Streeting risked becoming akin to David Mil...

item.title: US Waives Visa Bond Requirement for Certain World Cup Ticket Holders
item.description: The U.S. State Department has announced that travelers from Algeria, Cape Verde, Ivory Coast, Senegal, and Tunisia who purchased tickets for the 2026 FIFA World Cup will no longer need to post visa bo...

item.title: Air India to reduce 400 international flights temporarily due to high fuel costs and airspace restrictions
item.description: Air India has announced that it will reduce approximately 400 international flights per month from June to August across key regions including North America, Europe, Australia, and Southeast Asia. The...

item.title: Government to review Clean Electricity Regulations and expand electricity grid
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to unveil plans to transform Canada's electricity sector, aiming to double the nation's electricity grid. The government will review and amend the Clean Electricity R...

item.title: Nigeria’s Pension Assets Grow Slowly in March Amid Market Adjustments
item.description: For March 2026, Nigeria’s pension industry record small growth as market conditions don change and pension fund managers dey reposition their portfolios. After February wey pension assets jump by N1.3...

item.title: Economic Inequality Shapes Access to Medical Cannabis in the UK
item.description: In the United Kingdom, the legal status of cannabis as either a medicine or a criminal substance appears heavily influenced by a patient's financial means rather than medical necessity. Since 2018, sp...
